title: Quantum Key Distribution Demo
description: Build a simple BB84-style key-distribution simulation and show how measurement disturbance reveals eavesdropping.
whyItMatters: Quantum cryptography is one of the clearest places where the physics of information creates a new security model rather than a faster calculation.

steps.title: Implement sender and receiver basis choices
steps.description: Model the random basis-selection process and the raw key exchange.
steps.tips: Keep the logic visible so the security story stays understandable.

steps.title: Add sifting and error checking
steps.description: Compare compatible basis choices and estimate the observed error rate.
steps.tips: A clean classical post-processing step matters to the protocol.

steps.title: Simulate an eavesdropper
steps.description: Insert a simple intercept-resend attacker and observe how the statistics change.
steps.tips: The point is not fancy attack models; it is the measurement disturbance logic.

steps.title: Compare secure and insecure cases
steps.description: Show the raw and sifted key quality with and without the eavesdropper.
steps.tips: Use plots or tables, not just prose.

steps.title: Summarise what creates the security
steps.description: Explain why the protocol's security claim is about physics and noise detection rather than secrecy of the algorithm.
steps.tips: Be precise about ideal theory versus practical system limits.

rubric.description: The simulation captures the core basis-choice, sifting, and disturbance logic.
The write-up explains the physics-based security mechanism clearly.
Outputs make the difference between clean and attacked channels obvious.
